title: Bakeriella brevipes

This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bakeriella brevipes include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been reported to contribute to its medicinal properties. Bakeriella brevipes is commonly used in traditional medicine to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.

This family includes over 2,700 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. As part of the larger Euphorbiaceae family, Bakeriella brevipes is closely related to other species within this family, including those in the genera Plukenetia and Alchornea.
